title: Primitive Types 3
description: Arrays in Rust have a fixed size and contain elements of the same type. The type annotation is [Type; length].
instructions: Create an array with the correct syntax.
hints: Arrays are created with square brackets: [element1, element2, ...]
All elements must be the same type.
explanation: Arrays in Rust are fixed-size collections of elements of the same type. They are declared with square brackets, and their type is `[T; N]` where T is the element type and N is the length. Arrays are allocated on the stack and have a known size at compile time.
